title: [FIRRTL] Printed Type of Subfield Uses FieldID, Field Name Would be Better
body:
FIRRTL Dialect prints the accessor of a subfield as the field ID. While this matches the internal representation, this would be more readable if it showed the field name.

Consider:

```
circuit MuxChain :
  module MuxChain :
    input a: {a: UInt<1>}

    node a_a = a.a
```

This is printed as:

```mlir
module {
  firrtl.circuit "MuxChain"  {
    firrtl.module @MuxChain(in %a: !firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) {
      %0 = firrtl.subfield %a(0) : (!firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
      %a_a = firrtl.node interesting_name %0  : !firrtl.uint<1>
    }
  }
}
```

This would be more readable as:
```mlir
%0 = firrtl.subfield %a(a) : (!firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```

Or:

```mlir
%0 = firrtl.subfield %a.a : (!firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```

Or:

```mlir
%0 = firrtl.subfield %a("a") : (!firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```

h/t @rwy7 for the suggestion.
